key_insight: Plasma-sprayed nano Al2O3–TiO2 coatings, especially 4 wt.% Al2O3 + 2 wt.% TiO2, substantially improve corrosion resistance of AISI 1020 steel in NaCl environments.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies coating composition and salt concentration as the dominant factors affecting corrosion performance, guiding optimization of thermal-spray protection for mild steel components.
method: Definitive Screening Design (DSD) to vary Al2O3 content, NaCl concentration, exposure time, temperature, and pH.
Potentiodynamic polarization and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) on a Corrtest CS350M workstation.
Statistical modeling to rank factor influence and predict corrosion responses.
evidence_signal: The best coating achieved a corrosion current density of 2.18 µA cm−2 and a corrosion rate of 0.010 mm yr−1, with model fit reported at R² > 0.99.
watch_item: Results are specific to the tested plasma-sprayed nano Al2O3–TiO2 composition and electrochemical conditions, so durability under real service wear, thermal cycling, or long-term exposure still needs validation.
paper_title: STUDY ON THE CORROSION BEHAVIOUR OF PLASMA SPRAYED NANO Al2O3–TiO2– AISI 1020 STEEL COATINGS USING DEFINITIVE SCREENING DESIGN
